source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Electrical Cabinet DBU 7

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Green Canyon Block 627

event.top_event: Arc flash in electrical cabinet DBU 7
actions_taken: Smoke alarms activated and personnel mustered
Fire team assembled and entered port 690 V switchboard room
Third party contractor hired to inspect, repair, and replace damaged equipment
Rig returned to full capacity operation
event.summary: On May 26, 2017, smoke alarms in the port 690 V switchboard room of the Diamond Ocean Blackhornet drillship triggered a required muster. An arc flash occurred in Dynamic Braking Unit (DBU) 7 due to blown fuses that had not been replaced since May 10, 2017. Carbon buildup in the fuses created a path for electrical current, enabling arcing through an IGBT switch. The arcing destroyed the fuse housing and jumped to the cabinet frame, causing an arc flash that destroyed the cabinet and activated smoke alarms. Estimated damage was $50,000.
recommendations: If DBU fuses blow in the future, the maintenance crew should replace them immediately to prevent reoccurrence

hazards.name: Accumulation of carbon in electrical fuses

threats.name: Failure to replace blown fuses immediately
Deferral of maintenance due to operational constraints
Lack of knowledge regarding carbon buildup risk in fuses

consequences.name: Structural damage to electrical cabinet DBU 7
Activation of smoke alarms and required personnel muster
Fire and smoke damage to electrical equipment

controls.name: Smoke alarm system in electrical room
controls.barrier_role: Detection and alarm of fire hazard
controls.lod_basis: Automatic detection system triggered before catastrophic failure

supporting_text: On the morning of May 26, 2017, five smoke alarms activated simultaneously in the port 690 V switchboard room, and the general alarm was sounded for personnel to muster.
While other personnel mustered, the fire team assembled in order to enter the port 690 V switchboard room.

controls.name: Permit to Work (PTW) system for maintenance activities
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to manage maintenance work and operational constraints
controls.lod_basis: Formal approval process for maintenance work

supporting_text: On May 13, 2017, a PTW was obtained to replace the blown fuses and address the shorted inverter on DBU 7, but it was rescinded because the Blow Out Preventers (BOPs) were unlatched in preparation to install the subsea tree.
The crew did not want to de-energize half of the drawworks motors while the BOPs were being suspended and held by the drawworks.

controls.name: Immediate replacement of blown fuses
controls.barrier_role: Preventive maintenance to eliminate electrical fault source

supporting_text: During the evening of May 10, 2017, the night maintenance crew received a report from the drilling crew that there was an electrical fault in the port 690 volt (V) drilling switchboard.
The maintenance crew decided to replace the fuses and address the shorted inverter at a later date, when the port 690 V switchboard could be shut down without interrupting operations.
It has been determined if the DBU fuses blow in the future, the maintenance crew should replace them immediately to prevent a reoccurrence of this incident.

controls.name: Training and knowledge on electrical fault hazards
controls.barrier_role: Competence and awareness of carbon buildup risk in fuses

supporting_text: Lack of knowledge: The maintenance crew failed to recognize the possibilty of carbon buildup in the blown fuses in DBU 7.

controls.name: Fire team response and ventilation
controls.barrier_role: Emergency response to contain and mitigate fire damage
controls.lod_basis: Rapid response to limit extent of damage

supporting_text: While other personnel mustered, the fire team assembled in order to enter the port 690 V switchboard room. Upon entry, they ventilated the room and began to assess damage.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
